calendar.matters:
PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar Aug-04-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Yulissa Zulaica, for Petitioner Millard Piercy
The Court stated that the Petition reflected an imperfect will, and noted that there was a lack of witnesses to establish the decedent's testamentary intent. Ms. Zulaica stated that a supplemental declaration had been filed on August 3, 2015 to address the Court's concerns. She stated that even if there were no will, the Petitioner as the decedent's surviving domestic partner would inherit the estate anyway. She stated that the only problem with the will was the omission of a declaration undet penalty of perjury, but that the will contained affirmations by the subscribing witnesses that the testator declared the will represented his testamentary intent, and the will was initialed by the decedent on every page in addition to being signed. She also noted that no objection to the petition had been filed by anyone. The Court granted the Petition for Spousal Property (order signed).

Judge: Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Craig Blackstone/Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not reported)
